entries.rationale: IndiaVotes captured the initially misdeclared result. Contemporary Nagaland reporting says the result was reviewed and corrected in favour of NDPP candidate Namri Nchang by 168 votes, which matches the LokDhaba row.

detail.note: Local constituency detail is manually adjudicated for this seat because IndiaVotes captured the initially misdeclared result. The corrected final result follows the LokDhaba-aligned row and contemporary Nagaland reporting.

entries.rationale: Multiple public result summaries align with the LokDhaba row for Tobu 2018, while the captured IndiaVotes detail appears stale and under-counted for both total votes and candidate totals.

detail.note: Local constituency detail is manually adjudicated for this seat because the captured IndiaVotes fragment under-counts Tobu 2018. The corrected local sheet follows the LokDhaba-aligned final result.

entries.rationale: The staged IndiaVotes Delhi 2019 Lok Sabha file carries corrupted winner and runner-up candidate labels while preserving the correct BJP/INC vote totals and margins. The LokDhaba row matches the published Delhi 2019 result surface for this seat, so the atlas resolves to the LokDhaba winner sheet.

The staged IndiaVotes Karimganj 2019 seat row is internally broken: the winner label is blank and the winner vote share collapses to 0.3 despite the total-vote denominator matching the LokDhaba row. The atlas resolves this seat to the LokDhaba result sheet.

The atlas resolves this seat to the LokDhaba result sheet.

Both sources agree on the winner and vote totals, but IndiaVotes collapses the historical faction label to a generic RPI code while LokDhaba preserves the RPG tag used in the staged overlap set. The atlas resolves the seat to the LokDhaba-aligned historical party label.

Published 2014 Andhra Pradesh constituency result pages align on Varupula Subbarao of YSRCP for Prathipadu, while the LokDhaba-aligned staged overlap appears to be mis-keyed to a different winner sheet. The atlas resolves this seat to the IndiaVotes row.

Published 2014 Andhra Pradesh result references align on Pulaparty Narayana Murty of TDP for Gannavaram, while the LokDhaba-aligned staged overlap appears to have crossed into a different winner sheet. The atlas resolves this seat to the IndiaVotes row.

The staged LokDhaba overlap for Embalam 2016 is internally inconsistent, collapsing turnout and total-vote volume far below the published constituency result. The atlas resolves this seat to the IndiaVotes row, which aligns with published Puducherry 2016 winner lists.

Both sources agree on P. Rajavelu as winner, but the LokDhaba overlap collapses the total-vote base to 13,428 while keeping the winner tally at 12,933, producing an implausible 96.31% winner share. The atlas resolves the seat to the IndiaVotes row for turnout and vote-share integrity.

The LokDhaba overlap for Udaipur 2008 is internally broken: the winner tally and total-vote count both lose an order of magnitude, dropping Gulab Chand Kataria from 65,706 votes to 6,570 and turnout from 57.5% to 7.88%. The atlas resolves the seat to the IndiaVotes row.

The staged IndiaVotes candidate sheet for Aurangabad 2009 sums to 731,147 votes, which matches the LokDhaba row exactly and not the lower IndiaVotes summary-row total of 598,332. The atlas resolves this seat to the LokDhaba totals.
